skill.name: lark-workflow-standup-report
skill.description: Creates a daily or weekly Lark standup report from calendar events and unfinished tasks.
skill.summary: Summarizes Lark calendar agendas and task lists into a structured standup report.
skill.author: larksuite

security_audit.summary: The static external command findings are partially confirmed as legitimate lark-cli examples that read calendar and task data after user authorization. The path traversal, weak cryptography, and high entropy findings appear to be false positives from Markdown links, Chinese text, and formatted documentation; no prompt injection or malicious intent was found.

medium_findings.title: External CLI Reads Personal Calendar and Task Data
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs use of lark-cli commands for calendar agenda and task retrieval. This is core functionality, but it can expose sensitive work schedule and task information to the AI context.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples directly call lark-cli calendar and task read operations. The risk is data exposure, not command injection, because the commands are documented and limited to authorized user data.

low_findings.title: Relative Documentation Links Flagged as Path Traversal
low_findings.description: The scanner flagged ../ links, but they point to sibling Lark skill documentation. No file read outside the skill family or arbitrary path construction is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact locations are Markdown references to known sibling documentation paths. There is no dynamic path input or filesystem write behavior.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Crypto and Entropy Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: No weak cryptographic operation, encoded payload, binary content, or encrypted content was found in SKILL.md. The alerts appear caused by non-English text and Markdown formatting.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of the flagged header found YAML metadata and a Chinese description only. No cryptographic API, hash function, or obfuscated payload is present.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Command Examples
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill contains shell command examples for lark-cli authentication, calendar agenda retrieval, and task retrieval. These commands are expected for the workflow but should be run only with user consent.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The shell commands are visible and directly tied to the documented workflow. They are not hidden, obfuscated, or parameterized with untrusted input.

content.value_statement: Teams lose time checking calendars and task lists before daily updates. This skill combines Lark agenda data and unfinished tasks into a concise standup report.
actual_capabilities: Runs Lark calendar agenda commands for today or a specified ISO 8601 date range.
Runs Lark task commands to retrieve unfinished tasks assigned to the current user.
Combines calendar events and tasks into a date-based standup summary.
Sorts events by start time and tasks by due date.
Maps RSVP status values into readable labels.
Highlights schedule conflicts and estimated free time.
limitations: Requires lark-cli and user authorization for calendar and task scopes.
Only supports user identity workflows, not app-only identity workflows.
Natural language dates must be converted before command execution.
Large task histories may need filtering to avoid excessive context use.

use_cases.title: Daily Personal Briefing
use_cases.description: Create a morning view of meetings, unfinished tasks, conflicts, and open time blocks.

use_cases.title: Team Standup Preparation
use_cases.description: Prepare a structured update before a daily standup using current calendar and task data.

use_cases.title: Weekly Schedule Review
use_cases.description: Review upcoming meetings and overdue work to plan priorities for the week.

prompt_templates.title: Today Summary
prompt_templates.prompt: Create my Lark standup report for today. Include meetings, unfinished tasks, conflicts, and free time.

prompt_templates.title: Tomorrow Planning
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a standup report for tomorrow from my Lark calendar and unfinished tasks. Use ISO 8601 dates for commands.

prompt_templates.title: Focused Task Report
prompt_templates.prompt: Summarize my Lark agenda for this date and include only tasks due by the end of that day.

prompt_templates.title: Weekly Conflict Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my Lark schedule and tasks for this work week. Group tasks by due date and call out meeting conflicts.

output_examples.input: Create my standup report for today.
output_examples.output: Today has three meetings, including one pending RSVP. Two unfinished tasks are due today. There is one overlap between the product review and planning meeting.

output_examples.input: Show tomorrow's agenda and tasks due by tomorrow night.
output_examples.output: Tomorrow includes a morning sync and an afternoon design review. One task is overdue, two tasks are due tomorrow, and late morning appears available.
The week has eight scheduled events and six open tasks. Tuesday afternoon has a conflict, while Thursday morning has the largest planning window.

best_practices: Authorize only the calendar and task scopes required by the workflow.
Use explicit ISO 8601 date ranges for non-today reports.
Filter tasks by due date when the account has a large task history.
anti_patterns: Do not run the workflow without confirming the target date range.
Do not include declined events when calculating busy time.
Do not paste full raw command output into shared standup notes.

faq.question: Does this skill require Lark CLI?
faq.answer: Yes. It depends on lark-cli commands for calendar and task data.

faq.question: What permissions are needed?
faq.answer: It needs calendar event read access and task read access for the authorized user.

faq.question: Can it understand natural language dates directly?
faq.answer: No. The workflow requires the AI to convert dates into ISO 8601 values before command execution.

faq.question: Does it modify calendar events or tasks?
faq.answer: No. The documented commands retrieve agenda and task information only.

faq.question: Can it summarize a full week?
faq.answer: Yes. Use a clear weekly date range and expect more filtering for large task lists.

faq.question: Is it safe for sensitive work data?
faq.answer: Use caution. The workflow reads personal schedule and task data into the AI context.
